compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Root cause PROVEN + reproduced: a vanished loose object poisons the shared index; the failed whole-index commit leaves the entry staged, so every later write cascades (invalid object/Error building trees). Fix: _recover_from_invalid_object (read-tree HEAD + re-add + retry), wired into stage_and_commit + batch. Vetted — adversarial code review: APPROVE/ship; prior-art sweep: acceptable-with-caveats, all guardrails met (git-annex uses the identical index-rebuild). Added observability guardrail (WARN naming any orphaned worktree path) per both reviews. RED tests mutation-checked; lint/typecheck/full store suite (311) green; concurrency test green x4 locally. Pushed as Gerrit change 762. Non-blocking follow-up 9f8e: reconcile the orphaned worktree file.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
A vanished git object poisons the SHARED index and CASCADES: `git commit` (whole-index) fails
`error: invalid object <sha> for '<path>'` / `error: Error building trees`, and because the
failed commit leaves the poisoned entry staged, EVERY subsequent write's commit fails
identically until the index is reset. This is the residual behind the enrich-prune
concurrency flake after Mode A (4f8d) and Mode B (ac26) were fixed (CI: `lost=28..50
swallowed_write_raises=28..50`, all on ONE object). Genesis: a loose object that vanishes
between `git add` and `git commit` (a background gc repack, or a partial/interrupted object
write under CI FS/memory pressure).

## Reproduction Steps
1. Baseline commit so HEAD exists. 2. Stage an event file, then delete its loose object
   (`objects/xx/rest`) — a vanished object. 3. A NEW rebar write's whole-index commit builds a
   tree from the index and fails `invalid object … Error building trees`; the next write
   fails identically (proven cascade — `tests/.../test_event_append_transient_retry.py`).

**Expected:** a locked write self-heals and no write is lost.
**Actual (pre-fix):** the poison persists (the per-path `_unstage` clears the CURRENT write's
path, never the earlier poison's) and wedges every later commit.

## Root Cause
`git write-tree` verifies each index blob EXISTS; a missing one fails the tree build. The
failed commit leaves the poisoned entry in the index. rebar's existing recovery only unstages
the current write's path, so a cross-path poison is never cleared → cascade.

## Fix
`event_append._recover_from_invalid_object`: on the `invalid object`/`Error building trees`
signature, `git read-tree HEAD` (reset the WHOLE index to HEAD — drops the poison regardless
of path; worktree untouched), re-`git add` this write's file(s) — regenerating a missing
object from the intact worktree file — and retry the commit. Non-destructive on a false
positive; bounded (one recovery attempt); emits a WARNING naming any orphaned worktree path.
Verified accepted pattern (git-annex regenerates its index identically); vetted by an
adversarial code review (APPROVE) and a prior-art sweep (acceptable-with-caveats, all met).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A cross-path vanished-object poison self-heals instead of cascading; a subsequent write
  also succeeds (RED test `test_cross_path_poisoned_index_self_heals`, mutation-checked).
- [ ] A write whose OWN object vanished regenerates it and commits
  (`test_own_vanished_object_is_regenerated`).
- [ ] The heal logs a WARNING naming any orphaned worktree path (observability guardrail).
- [ ] `make lint` + `make typecheck` pass; the enrich-prune concurrency test passes; no
  existing store test weakened.
compiled_state.title: Vanished git object poisons the shared index and cascades all writes (enrich-prune Mode D)
